evidence: QCi loses critical-load served and/or critical ENS to the best baseline in scenario-stress comparison. QCi max fraction customers unserved is higher than the best baseline. lowest QCi energy is best risk for 0/8 payloads and best critical-served for 0/8 payloads. All raw QCi samples violate constraints before repair, then repair projects them to feasible states; hardware objective does not directly see repaired feasibility/resilience. coefficient max=9.18e+05, median |coef|=8, median scale=1.9e-06. corr(qci_energy, critical_load_served_fraction)=0.086; lower QCi energy is not a reliable repaired-resilience ranking signal.
recommended_small_change: reweight critical ENS/max-unserved penalties, rerank QCi samples by repaired Phase 3 metrics, and rebuild QCi-fit patches using two-bus/full-132 patches before any new QCi run

evidence: QCi loses critical-load served and/or critical ENS to the best baseline in scenario-stress comparison. lowest QCi energy is best risk for 19/60 payloads and best critical-served for 36/60 payloads. All raw QCi samples violate constraints before repair, then repair projects them to feasible states; hardware objective does not directly see repaired feasibility/resilience. coefficient max=5.04e+04, median |coef|=8, median scale=0.0001. QCi-fit payloads solve single/two-microgrid patches rather than the full 198-variable source payload; median variable count is below the 132 limit. corr(qci_energy, critical_load_served_fraction)=0.756; lower QCi energy is not a reliable repaired-resilience ranking signal. repeat/sample budget is 30; increasing samples alone is secondary unless paired with repaired-metric reranking.
recommended_small_change: reweight critical ENS/max-unserved penalties, rerank QCi samples by repaired Phase 3 metrics, and rebuild QCi-fit patches using two-bus/full-132 patches before any new QCi run
